ungrounded.explanation: Cross-Border Finance Assistant handles its declared purpose; apply standard data handling and human review as appropriate. (No sources cited.)

grounded.explanation: Cross-Border Finance Assistant: policy constraints are grounded in specific public guidance — [nist-ai-rmf#nist-govern-accountability] NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI 100-1); [eu-ai-act#eu-art12-logging] EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) — selected articles; [eu-ai-act#eu-art10-data] EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) — selected articles; [cpoa-fixture-docs#fx-healthcare-phi] ClearPoint Workforce Agent — Test Agent Zoo business stories (§6.6). Each proposed control (owner accountability, data boundaries, human oversight, and record-keeping) maps to a cited source rather than a generic summary.

grounding_refs.snippet: The GOVERN function calls for clear roles, responsibilities, and lines of accountability for AI systems. Each system should have an accountable owner so that risk decisions and remediation have a responsible party. An agent with no accountable owner cannot be governed.

Article 12 requires high-risk AI systems to automatically record events (logs) over their lifecycle to ensure traceability of functioning. A tamper-evident evidence trail of the onboarding decision supports this record-keeping obligation.

Article 10 sets data governance expectations for high-risk AI, including relevance, representativeness, and appropriate handling of personal data. Explicit data-class boundaries, retention caps, and approval for external sharing of regulated data support these expectations.

grounding_refs.source_title: ClearPoint Workforce Agent — Test Agent Zoo business stories (§6.6)
grounding_refs.snippet: Healthcare support agent with regulated PHI exposure and a ticketing workflow. Requires data boundary, retention cap, approval for external sharing, and elevated evidence. Expected decision: Ready with Conditions.
